title: galarhoeus pekinensis var. hupehensis
intro: Galarhoeus pekinensis var. hupehensis, also known as Chinese milkweed or Hubei galarhoeus, is a plant species in the family Euphorbiaceae. It has been traditionally used in Chinese medicine for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating snakebites and other external injuries, as well as its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The plant's roots, leaves, and stems have been used to prepare various herbal remedies, including infusions, decoctions, and poultices, which are believed to offer health benefits such as reducing fever, relieving pain, and promoting wound healing. In addition to its medicinal uses, the plant's roots have been used in traditional Chinese medicine to treat various health conditions, including rheumatism, skin diseases, and digestive issues.
